patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 7's sub-0.02 hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap band remains genuinely disputed: it false-negatived winners on 7/10, 7/12, 7/18, 7/19, and 7/21 (CHW@TEX won 10-0 after being DOWNGRADEd), yet the one real-stake OVERRIDE of this exact cap (7/20 CHW@TEX, citing deGrom as an offsetting ace) lost 10-3, and the very next blanket 'prior-day-loss' DOWNGRADE rule (7/21) missed another 10-run TEX win. Applied today by deferring to the cap on NYM@MIL (gap=+0.0001) and CHW@TEX (gap=+0.0060) without inventing a directional override or a standing rule from a single prior day.

HARD CAP 10's CONFIRM-rescue mechanism is net-unprofitable in the archive: the prompt's own documentation shows CONFIRM-rescued bottom-bucket picks hit only 47.6% (below breakeven), and 7/21's three real-stake rescues went 1-for-3 (SD@ATL WIN with F3=2777.6 and full 4-dimension agreement; SF@KC LOSS and DET@CHC LOSS with weaker/inconsistent support). Applied today by NOT rescuing SF@KC (F3=735.3, the identical matchup to yesterday's rescue loss), SD@ATL (no F-signal firing, pre-cap score only 2), or MIN@CLE (CAP7 already legitimately fires independent of CAP10).

Dual-opposing SP-xERA + PQI signals (both against the pick) continue to be validated as stake-suppression-only, never a directional fade, across roughly 15 tracked instances from 7/17-7/21 regardless of which direction the opposition pointed. Applied today by holding SKIP (not fading) on OAK@ARI, CIN@SEA, LAD@PHI, TB@TOR, and DET@CHC.

HARD CAP 1 (negative-edge GOLD, PIT@NYY G1 edge=-4.41pp) and HARD CAP 6 (extreme positive-edge hallucination, PIT@NYY G2 edge=+28.17pp) both fire today. The brain prompt is explicit both caps are non-negotiable despite the archive flagging 3 consecutive CAP-1-suppressed score-4+ winners (TB@BOS, MIA@MIL, NYM@MIL on 7/18-20) — deferred to both without relitigating, per the prompt's standing guidance not to relax validated caps.

Two PIT@NYY-family and one BAL@BOS game were already in progress at the time of this run (PIT leading NYY 3-0; BOS leading BAL 6-2; SF@KC 0-0 early) per live statsapi data. Neither changes the underlying cap-driven decisions, but is noted as live color where relevant.

The brain prompt treats CAP 1 as non-negotiable (validated 4-for-5 live) despite the archive flagging 3 recent instances where it suppressed score-4+ winners (7/18-7/20). Live: this game is already in progress with PIT leading 3-0. Defer to the cap.

PIT @ NYY G2.reasoning: HARD CAP 6 fired on edge=+28.17pp (>25pp), the calibrator-hallucination range, collapsing a pre-cap A (score 5) to C/score0 despite F-signal, SP edge (1.51 xERA), and PQI (Δ=+18.3) all agreeing with NYY. F1_xera_gap=1.31* is starred (thin sample), adding independent caution. CAP 6 is validated 3-for-3 in the archive and not to be relitigated. Defer to the cap.

BAL @ BOS G1.reasoning: Model's own pipeline already tiers this SKIP (grade B/score2, team_quality modifier disabled) with no hard cap needed — edge 6.19pp is in-band but the score never cleared elevation. Game is in progress with BOS already leading 6-2, consistent with the pick direction. Nothing in recent postgame history argues for elevating a pick the model itself declined to stake.

SF @ KC.reasoning: SF was demoted DIAMOND->GOLD by the rolling-benchmark guardrail, then HARD CAP 10 zeroed a strong pre-cap A (score5: F-signal fires, SP edge 2.12 agrees, PQI Δ=-32.3 confirms, Stage agree) because pick_prob sits in the bottom bucket. This is the identical matchup to 7/21, where a similarly strong-looking CAP10-rescue CONFIRM (F3=822.2) lost 2-3. Today's F3=735.3 is again sub-1000. Given the fresh same-matchup precedent, do not rescue.

NYM @ MIL.reasoning: HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0001, the most extreme example yet of the disputed sub-0.02 false-negative band (winners suppressed 7/10, 7/12, 7/18, 7/19). But the one real-stake OVERRIDE of this cap type (7/20 CHW@TEX) lost 10-3, and F3=646.4 here is below the 1000 meaningful-anchor threshold (this exact matchup's 7/21 DOWNGRADE was correctly protective on a thin F3=243.9 stack). No starred F1 signal is present, so the F1*-without-F3 caution doesn't apply here. Sound pick with no suppression rationale.

Per the settled archive rule (validated across ~15 instances 7/17-7/21), dual-opposing signals are stake-suppression only, never a directional fade — hold SKIP without fading toward OAK.
